toronto.planningContext: Toronto building means working narrow downtown lots, century-old homes in pockets like Cabbagetown and Riverdale, and the city's recent multiplex-as-of-right rules that opened up fourplex conversions across most residential streets. Site logistics, neighbour notices, and protected trees usually shape the schedule as much as the design itself.
toronto.projectFit: We are a strong fit for owners planning a downtown custom rebuild, a fourplex or laneway-suite conversion, a heritage-respectful renovation, or a full gut on a tight lot. You get one accountable team handling design, permits, and construction so the timeline actually holds.
toronto.approvalFocus: Confirm your zoning category, maximum gross floor area, and whether your project clears as-of-right or needs a Committee of Adjustment minor variance before you commit to a design. Tree protection, laneway access, and Toronto Building's two-stage permit review (zoning then building) are the items that quietly delay most projects.

custom-homes.serviceScope: Toronto custom homes mostly happen on 20-30 foot lots in Riverdale, Leaside, and East York, where staging space is tight and party-wall coordination with attached neighbours is part of every schedule. Owners pushing past as-of-right massing in Lytton Park or Davisville Village should expect a Committee of Adjustment minor variance and a longer pre-permit runway.
custom-homes.shortAnswer: We run Toronto custom builds end-to-end — feasibility, architectural drawings, structural and zoning approvals, then construction through occupancy — with one project manager owning every milestone.
custom-homes.introNote: Most Toronto custom home projects start with a teardown on a tight 20-35 foot lot in Cabbagetown, Leaside, or East York, where the real work is fitting a livable program inside as-of-right limits before deciding whether a Committee of Adjustment variance is worth the timeline cost. Vitalite handles the full sequence — zoning analysis, drawings, two-stage Toronto Building permit review, tree protection, and grading — so the schedule reflects the actual 12-16 month path to occupancy.
custom-homes.readinessNote: Before drawings begin, owners should pull the lot's zoning category, FSI, height and setback caps, and a current survey to test whether the wishlist clears as-of-right or needs a minor variance. Inspiration images, target square footage, budget direction, and any known mature trees or grading concerns let Vitalite price a realistic scope before money is spent on design.
custom-homes.helpNote: Vitalite coordinates the Toronto-specific pieces other builders miss — Committee of Adjustment notices where needed, Toronto Urban Forestry tree permits, two-stage Toronto Building submission, and grading sign-off through Toronto Water — with one project manager tracking every comment and trade from concept through final occupancy.

garden-suites.serviceScope: Garden suites land best on the deeper lots of East York, Riverdale, and the older Beaches blocks where rear yards still have 25 feet of usable depth behind the main house. We coordinate Toronto's garden suite zoning review, servicing routing through existing yards, and tree protection for mature canopies that often dictate the final footprint.
garden-suites.shortAnswer: On qualifying Toronto lots, we design and build a permit-ready garden suite as a self-contained backyard rental or family unit, handling zoning checks, servicing, and the build under one fixed scope.
garden-suites.introNote: Toronto garden suites work best on the continuous laneway blocks of Roncesvalles, Leslieville, and the Annex where fire access can be confirmed and rear yards still have meaningful depth behind the main house. Vitalite runs the feasibility check first — laneway frontage, TRCA ravine status near the Don and Humber, tree protection, and servicing distance — so owners know whether the lot is buildable before any design fees are spent.
garden-suites.readinessNote: Owners should pull a current survey, confirm laneway access width and fire route compliance, and check whether the rear yard sits inside a TRCA-regulated area near the Don or Humber valleys. A clear sense of intended use — long-term rental, family suite, or future resale — helps Vitalite size the unit and finishes against Toronto's garden suite zoning before drawings start.
garden-suites.helpNote: Vitalite handles the Toronto Building garden suite zoning review, fire access confirmation with Toronto Fire Services, TRCA clearance where applicable, servicing trench routing through tight rear yards, and the construction itself — one fixed scope from feasibility through final inspection so the rental is income-ready on handover.

multiplex.serviceScope: Toronto's multiplex-as-of-right rules opened the door to fourplex conversions and ground-up four-unit builds across most residential streets in Cabbagetown, Riverdale, and the Annex. We work the numbers around unit mix, fire separations, and parking relief so the project pencils before drawings begin.
multiplex.shortAnswer: For Toronto multiplex projects, we deliver as-of-right fourplex conversions and new builds — financial feasibility, drawings, permits, and construction — with rental-grade specs that protect long-term yield.
multiplex.introNote: Toronto's as-of-right fourplex zoning across most residential streets has shifted Parkdale, East York, and Scarborough into the GTA's most active multiplex markets, with investors converting bungalows and semis into four legal rental units. Vitalite builds the pro forma around realistic unit mix, fire separation costs, and stormwater upgrades so the numbers hold before drawings begin.
multiplex.readinessNote: Investors should bring the lot dimensions, current zoning, target rent assumptions, and a clear capital stack before design starts so Vitalite can stress-test the as-of-right fourplex math against build cost. Existing service connections, combined-sewer capacity, and parking relief options on the block are the items that most often shift the project economics late in the process.
multiplex.helpNote: Vitalite delivers Toronto multiplex projects under the as-of-right rules — financial feasibility, fourplex drawings with code-compliant fire separations and egress, Toronto Building permit submission, parking and stormwater coordination, and rental-grade construction — with one team protecting yield from concept through tenant handover.

home-additions.serviceScope: Most Toronto additions are second-storey pop-ups and rear extensions on Cabbagetown semis, Riverdale Victorians, and Leaside brick homes where lot widths sit between 16 and 30 feet. Underpinning, party-wall notices, and angular plane setbacks tend to drive the structural approach more than the wishlist does.
home-additions.shortAnswer: We deliver Toronto additions — rear, side, second-storey, or full underpinning — as a single design-build contract so structural, mechanical, and finishing trades stay in step.
home-additions.introNote: Toronto additions typically work the constraints of 20-30 foot lots in Cabbagetown, Riverdale, and Leaside — angular plane setbacks, attached party walls, and rear yard depth shape what is buildable long before the wishlist does. Vitalite scopes second-storey pop-ups, rear extensions, and basement underpinning as one contract so structural and trade sequencing line up cleanly.
home-additions.readinessNote: Owners should pull a survey, confirm the existing foundation type, and review whether the addition triggers angular plane or lot coverage limits before committing to a footprint. Knowing whether the family plans to live through the build, the priority rooms, and any party-wall conditions with attached neighbours lets Vitalite plan the structural and staging approach early.
home-additions.helpNote: Vitalite coordinates the Toronto pieces additions usually need — angular plane and zoning review, party-wall notices for semis, underpinning engineering, Toronto Urban Forestry tree clearances, and Toronto Building permit submission — with one project manager keeping structural, mechanical, and finishing trades in sequence through occupancy.

north-york.planningContext: North York is dominated by 50-by-120 foot post-war lots in Willowdale, Bayview Village, and Don Valley Village where 1950s-60s bungalows are being replaced with modern two-storey customs or expanded into legal multiplexes. Lot widths and Bayview/Yonge corridor traffic make staging and crane access real planning items, not afterthoughts.
north-york.projectFit: We are well suited to Willowdale and Newtonbrook teardowns, York Mills and Hogg's Hollow estate-scale customs, second-storey additions on bungalows, and basement suites built to legal-rental standards. Owners get a fixed scope, transparent pricing, and a single point of contact from drawings through final occupancy.
north-york.approvalFocus: Check angular planes, side-yard setbacks, and lot-coverage caps early since North York's residential zones are stricter than many owners expect. Toronto Urban Forestry tree permits and grading/stormwater approval through Toronto Water are the two items most likely to push your start date if left until last.

custom-homes.serviceScope: Most North York custom homes replace a tired 1950s-60s bungalow in Willowdale, Newtonbrook, or Bayview Village with a 4,000-6,000 sq ft two-storey on the original 50x120 lot. Estate-scale rebuilds in York Mills and Hogg's Hollow run larger and longer because of ravine setbacks, slope work, and TRCA approvals.
custom-homes.shortAnswer: If you're rebuilding a North York bungalow into a modern family home, we manage feasibility through occupancy — drawings, FSI math, tree permits, and the build itself — under one design-build contract.
custom-homes.introNote: North York custom homes almost always start with a 1950s-60s bungalow on a 50x120 lot in Willowdale or Newtonbrook, replaced with a 4,000-6,000 sq ft two-storey, or with an estate-scale rebuild in York Mills and Hogg's Hollow. Vitalite shapes the design around North York's strict angular plane and lot coverage rules so the program lands cleanly inside Toronto Building's two-stage review.
custom-homes.readinessNote: Owners should secure a survey, the lot's R-zone classification, and a tree inventory before drawings begin since front-yard maples in Willowdale routinely set the buildable footprint. A target square footage, primary suite location, and basement use case let Vitalite test the FSI math and angular plane against the wishlist before committing to a design.
custom-homes.helpNote: Vitalite handles North York's specific approvals — Toronto Urban Forestry tree clearance on front-yard maples, angular plane and lot coverage review, TRCA on ravine-edge York Mills lots, and Toronto Water grading sign-off — with one project manager owning the two-stage permit and trade schedule through occupancy.

garden-suites.serviceScope: North York's deep 120-foot lots in Willowdale and Bayview Village are some of the GTA's better candidates for garden suites, particularly where existing detached garages can be replaced with a code-compliant rear unit. Servicing distance from the main house and Toronto Urban Forestry tree clearances usually drive the design constraints.
garden-suites.shortAnswer: For North York owners with a deep lot, we run a feasibility check then deliver a permit-ready garden suite — zoning review, drawings, servicing, and build — with rental-grade finishes.
garden-suites.introNote: North York's deep 120-foot lots in Willowdale, Bayview Village, and parts of Newtonbrook are among the GTA's better garden suite candidates, particularly where an existing detached garage can come down and be replaced with a code-compliant rear unit. Vitalite runs the tree clearance and servicing trench check first since front-yard maples typically gate the permit before drawings begin.
garden-suites.readinessNote: Owners should pull a current survey with rear yard depth, locate existing service connections from the main house, and budget for Toronto Urban Forestry to clear front-yard trees before any garden suite permit moves. Clarity on intended use — rental income, multigenerational, or future resale — lets Vitalite size the suite against North York's zoning and lot coverage limits early.
garden-suites.helpNote: Vitalite manages the North York-specific items — Toronto Urban Forestry tree clearance on front-yard canopy, garden suite zoning review with Toronto Building, servicing routing across long rear yards, and rental-grade construction — under one fixed scope from feasibility through final inspection.

multiplex.serviceScope: Many Willowdale and Lansing lots now support a four-unit multiplex new build under Toronto's city-wide as-of-right zoning, replacing a single bungalow with stacked rental units that hold parking on grade. Unit mix, fire separations, and storm drainage upgrades on combined-sewer streets are the numbers that make or break the pro forma.
multiplex.shortAnswer: We design and build North York multiplexes — typically four-unit new builds on 50-foot Willowdale or Bayview Village lots — with the financial model, permits, and construction handled in one accountable contract.
multiplex.introNote: Willowdale and Newtonbrook 50-foot lots have become some of Toronto's most workable as-of-right fourplex sites, replacing a single bungalow with four stacked rental units and parking on grade. Vitalite runs the multiplex pro forma against North York's actual lot coverage, FSI, and combined-sewer realities so the project pencils before drawings start.
multiplex.readinessNote: Investors should bring the lot dimensions, target rent assumptions, financing terms, and existing service connection details so Vitalite can model the as-of-right fourplex against build cost. Combined-sewer capacity on older Willowdale streets often forces a stormwater upgrade that needs pricing in from day one rather than discovered at permit comment stage.
multiplex.helpNote: Vitalite delivers the full North York multiplex package — feasibility math, fourplex drawings, fire separation and egress engineering, Toronto Building permit submission, stormwater and combined-sewer coordination through Toronto Water, and rental-grade construction — with one team accountable from concept through tenant handover.

home-additions.serviceScope: North York additions are usually second-storey pop-ups on Willowdale and Newtonbrook bungalows, plus rear extensions in Bayview Village to gain a primary suite or family room. Existing foundation capacity, angular plane limits, and a fresh grading plan are the structural items that get reviewed first.
home-additions.shortAnswer: We add second storeys, rear extensions, and finished basement suites to North York bungalows — one team handling structural design, permits, and construction so the home stays liveable through the build where possible.
home-additions.introNote: Most North York additions are second-storey pop-ups on Willowdale and Newtonbrook bungalows, often combined with a rear extension to gain a primary suite or larger kitchen. Vitalite confirms the existing foundation can carry a new storey and tests the design against North York's strict angular plane and lot coverage caps before structural drawings begin.
home-additions.readinessNote: Owners should commission a foundation review, gather a current survey, and verify the lot coverage and angular plane caps for their R-zone before pricing the addition. A clear sense of which rooms are non-negotiable, whether the family plans to live through the build, and any drainage issues with neighbouring lots help Vitalite set realistic structural and grading expectations.
home-additions.helpNote: Vitalite coordinates the North York-specific reviews — angular plane and lot coverage analysis, foundation underpinning where needed, Toronto Urban Forestry tree clearance, and Toronto Water grading sign-off — with one project manager keeping structural, mechanical, and finishing trades in sequence through final occupancy.

markham.planningContext: Markham work tends to fall into two camps: high-end customs and luxury renovations on Cachet, Bayview Glen, and Angus Glen estate lots, and additions or basement suites in established Unionville and Thornhill streets. The City's design review and heritage rules in old Unionville and Thornhill Village add a layer most other GTA municipalities do not have.
markham.projectFit: We deliver design-build customs and major additions for owners in Cachet, Angus Glen, and Berczy, plus permit-ready renovations and legal second-suite work for landlords. If your home sits inside a heritage conservation district, we manage the design review submission alongside the building permit so nothing stalls.
markham.approvalFocus: Markham reviews lot grading and stormwater more strictly than many GTA cities, so secure your grading plan and TRCA clearance (where applicable) before drawings go in. Heritage permit timing in Unionville Heritage Conservation District should be confirmed up front since it runs in parallel to, not inside, the building permit.

custom-homes.serviceScope: Markham custom homes range from 5,000-8,000 sq ft estate builds in Cachet and Angus Glen on half-acre lots to teardown-rebuilds replacing 1980s housing stock in Berczy and Bullock. Site plan control, design review, and the City's stricter grading review add steps that other 905 municipalities skip.
custom-homes.shortAnswer: For Markham custom homes, we run estate-scale projects in Cachet and Angus Glen from concept through final inspection — architecture, engineering, City approvals, and trades all coordinated by one team.
custom-homes.introNote: Markham custom homes are typically estate-scale builds on Cachet, Angus Glen, or Berczy lots in the 5,000-8,000 sq ft range, where the City's design review process adds an iteration cycle most other 905 municipalities skip. Vitalite plans for that review up front so the program, massing, and exterior treatment hold through Markham's design and grading approvals.
custom-homes.readinessNote: Owners should confirm whether the lot sits inside a registered subdivision with architectural control, gather a current survey and grading plan, and clarify whether Unionville Heritage Conservation District applies before drawings start. A target square footage, exterior style direction, and budget envelope let Vitalite plan against Markham's design review and stricter grading workup early.
custom-homes.helpNote: Vitalite coordinates the Markham-specific approvals — design review submission, site plan control where applicable, the City's detailed grading and stormwater review, Heritage permit work in Unionville HCD, and TRCA where relevant — with one project manager owning the schedule from feasibility through final occupancy.

garden-suites.serviceScope: Markham's wider lots in Cachet, Berczy, and parts of Unionville often support a detached secondary suite where servicing depth and tree protection allow, though the City's review is more conservative than Toronto's. We map zoning, parking, and lot grading constraints up front so the design fits the rules instead of fighting them.
garden-suites.shortAnswer: On qualifying Markham estate lots, we design and build a permit-ready garden suite that meets the City's secondary suite rules — feasibility, drawings, and construction in one contract.
garden-suites.introNote: Markham garden suites work differently than Toronto's because most lots have no laneway access, so rear yard suites depend on servicing trench routing through the main yard and Markham's confirmation that the City can deliver water, sewer, and electrical to the rear unit. Vitalite runs that servicing feasibility check before drawings begin since it dictates whether the suite is viable at all.
garden-suites.readinessNote: Owners should pull a survey showing rear yard depth, locate existing service connections, and budget for the City's servicing capacity confirmation before any garden suite design starts. A clear use case — rental income, family member, or short-term flexibility — helps Vitalite size the unit against Markham's secondary suite zoning and parking rules.
garden-suites.helpNote: Vitalite manages the Markham-specific items — servicing capacity confirmation with the City, garden suite zoning and parking review, lot grading workup, and tree protection — under one fixed scope from feasibility through code-compliant rental-grade construction.

multiplex.serviceScope: Markham's appetite for multiplex sits mainly in legal basement suites and additional residential units rather than four-unit conversions, with stronger demand for one or two suites inside an existing or new custom home in Unionville and Thornhill. Egress, ceiling height, and separate servicing routes are the items the City scrutinizes most closely.
multiplex.shortAnswer: In Markham we focus on legal second suites and ARU builds inside single-family homes, handling Building Code-compliant separations, separate entrances, and permit submission as one package.
multiplex.introNote: Markham's zoning is more restrictive than Toronto's so the multiplex path here is mostly legal secondary suites and ARUs inside single-family homes rather than four-unit conversions, with Unionville and Thornhill seeing the most activity. Vitalite scopes second-suite work with separate egress, fire separation, and servicing routes designed to clear Markham's permit review on first submission.
multiplex.readinessNote: Owners should confirm the existing basement ceiling height, foundation type, and service entrance capacity before committing to a secondary suite layout since these usually drive the cost spread. A clear sense of long-term use — rental, multigenerational, or resale-ready — lets Vitalite test the suite plan against Markham's secondary suite zoning and Building Code requirements.
multiplex.helpNote: Vitalite delivers Markham second suites and ARUs as one package — Building Code-compliant fire separation and ceiling height, separate egress, separate servicing routing, parking review, and Markham building permit submission — with rental-grade finishes ready for tenant on handover.

home-additions.serviceScope: Markham additions tend to be rear extensions and second-storey expansions on 1980s-90s homes in Unionville, Cathedraltown, and Berczy, where families want a primary suite or larger kitchen without leaving the school catchment. The City asks for a full grading and tree review on most of these, which we package with the building permit.
home-additions.shortAnswer: We deliver Markham additions — second storeys, rear extensions, and basement reworks — coordinating Markham's site plan and grading review alongside the construction so the schedule actually holds.
